process_name: LFU ratio in B -> D tau nu over B -> D light-lepton nu
implementation_difficulty_reason: A live R(D) constraint needs new charged-current semileptonic operators, B -> D form-factor or integrated-rate machinery, and a correlated R(D)-R(D*) likelihood; none of this is present in the current Delta F=2 or mu->e gamma surfaces.

required_grep_commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd scripts tests docs -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd scripts tests docs -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "MODERN_PHENOMENOLOGY_SYSTEM_IDS|DEFAULT_DELTA_F2_INPUTS|evaluate_bd_mixing|evaluate_bs_mixing|evaluate_d0_mixing|check_mu_to_e_gamma" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams tests docs -g '!*.ipynb'
targeted_grep_commands: rg -n -F "R(D)" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
rg -n -F "B -> D" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
rg -n -F "B->D" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
rg -n -F "D tau" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
rg -n -F "b -> c" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
rg -n -i -F "charged Higgs" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
rg -n -i -F "leptoquark" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
evidence: Required broad greps found the existing Delta F=2 and mu->e gamma surfaces, but no R(D) implementation.
Targeted fixed-string searches over the required implementation directories returned no B -> D tau nu, b -> c charged-current, charged-Higgs, or leptoquark observable hits.
R_D fixed-string hits were identifier substrings such as PAPER_*_ID in Delta F=2 infrastructure, not LFU-ratio code.

pdg_values.label: HFLAV semileptonic B averages, CKM 2025
pdg_values.observable: R(D) = B(B -> D tau nu_tau) / B(B -> D l nu_l), l=e,mu

status_history.reason: Initial PKA source extraction and draft skeleton for B025.

CA batch ca_w23_beauty verified CHK-1 through CHK-8.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(R_D\equiv
\mathcal{B}(B\to D\tau\nu_\tau)/\mathcal{B}(B\to D\ell\nu_\ell)\), with
\(\ell=e,\mu\).  This beauty-family entry covers the tau-versus-light-lepton
charged-current LFU ratio in exclusive \(b\to c\ell\nu\) semileptonic decays.
sections.pdg_prose:
The canonical current equivalent value is the HFLAV CKM 2025 joint average,
updated on 2025-09-28 and snapshotted in
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/B025/hflav\_ckm2025\_rdrds.txt}:
\[
  R_D = 0.358 \pm 0.024 .
\]
The source key is \texttt{hflav\_ckm2025\_rdrds}.  The same fit gives
\(R_{D^*}=0.281\pm0.011\), correlation
\(\rho(R_D,R_{D^*})=-0.374\), and \(\chi^2/\mathrm{dof}=16.683/14\)
with confidence level \(0.27\).  For comparison, HFLAV quotes an arithmetic
SM reference \(R_D=0.296\pm0.004\), \(R_{D^*}=0.254\pm0.005\).  Against that
reference the combined \(R_D\)-\(R_{D^*}\) discrepancy is quoted as about
\(3.8\sigma\), with \(p=1.48\times10^{-4}\).  The sidecar records the
individual \(R_D\) inputs from BaBar, Belle, LHCb, and Belle II.
sections.relevance:
\(R_D\) probes \(b\to c\tau\nu\) charged currents rather than neutral
\(\Delta F=2\) mixing.  It is therefore not a direct constraint on the current
quark-scan lane, but it is important for any warped/anarchic extension with
non-universal charged currents, third-generation enhanced lepton couplings,
charged scalar exchange, \(W'\)-like vectors, or leptoquark-motivated matching.
The ratio cancels many normalization inputs, so a persistent excess is a clean
diagnostic of LFU violation once the model supplies the relevant semileptonic
Wilson coefficients and neutrino-flavor assumptions.
sections.post_2008:
The Csaki--Falkowski--Weiler 2008 baseline predates the \(R_D^{(*)}\) anomaly
program.  The key post-2008 shift was BaBar's 2012 and 2013 evidence for an
excess in \(B\to D^{(*)}\tau\nu\), followed by Belle hadronic-tag and
semileptonic-tag measurements, LHCb measurements with muonic tau decays, and
Belle II semileptonic-tag and CKM-2025 hadronic-tag inputs.  HFLAV now uses a
joint correlated average rather than a single-experiment comparison.  On the
SM side, lattice and form-factor work matured substantially: HFLAV references
FNAL/MILC, HPQCD, JLQCD, FLAG 2024, and later phenomenological combinations,
so the tension is now limited by correlated experimental averaging and form
factor conventions rather than by the absence of a basic SM prediction.
sections.validity:
The experimental ratio is robust, but interpreting it as an RS bound is
model-dependent.  A live constraint needs the operator basis for
\((\bar c\,\Gamma b)(\bar\tau\,\Gamma\nu)\), the assumed neutrino species, and
the integrated \(B\to D\) form-factor response to vector, scalar, and tensor
operators.  Because HFLAV fits \(R_D\) together with \(R_{D^*}\), a serious
implementation should use the two-dimensional likelihood or covariance rather
than a standalone Gaussian for \(R_D\).
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  The PKA reran the required coverage greps over
\texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/}, \texttt{flavorConstraints/},
\texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/}, \texttt{warpConfig/},
\texttt{solvers/}, \texttt{scanParams/}, and \texttt{tests/}.  The broad plan
greps recover the existing neutral-meson \(\Delta F=2\) and
\(\mu\to e\gamma\) surfaces, but no \(R_D\), \(B\to D\tau\nu\), \(b\to c\)
charged-current, charged-Higgs, or leptoquark observable implementation.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  Promotion to a live constraint requires a new semileptonic
charged-current observable, form-factor/mode integration for at least the
\(B\to D\) final state, and a correlated treatment with \(R_{D^*}\).  This
belongs in the modern constraint surface if it is later implemented.
